description: Higher modulator concentration can slow nucleation and support gel window up to saturation risk.
Intermediate concentration favours network formation; too low favours isolated crystals.
Moderate temperatures balance nucleation and aggregation; very high values can favor powder/ripening.
Longer times can support gel aging, but may also allow recrystallization.
Supercritical or slow exchange drying reduces capillary collapse risk.
Larger meso/interparticle pores reduce capillary pressure in the Young-Laplace heuristic.
Lower surface tension reduces drying stress.
Contact angle modulates cos(theta) in capillary pressure.
Expert descriptor for hydrolytic/mechanical framework stability.
